L1.area: The vulnerability resides in the platform's class-document rendering infrastructure — the template layer that governs how wiki pages associated with structured data objects are displayed, including how those templates include and render embedded page content.

L2.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists in the platform's content-inclusion mechanism: when a privileged template includes content from a user-editable document, the included content executes with the template author's elevated privileges rather than the document author's own permissions. An authenticated low-privileged user can inject scripting macros into their own document, and the platform executes them under the privileged author context, enabling arbitrary code execution and full privilege escalation across the wiki instance.

ground_truth.reason: The XWiki ClassSheet include directive executes included content with the rights of the ClassSheet author rather than the target document's author, allowing any user to escalate privileges by binding the ClassSheet to their document and injecting scripting macros.
